Business Manager Job at Filmhouse Cinemas Limited




        
    

        
        Filmhouse Cinemas Limited is a dynamic film exhibition company. Our expertise are in the areas of: Cinema operations, management of sites, Ownership of sites, Consultancy for cinema development, Design Fit-out; Cinema project management, Film Distribution: Rights Acquisition, Booking into cinemas, and content development.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Manager

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Job type: Full time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Responsible for interpreting and implementing strategic cinema business plan.
	Manage the entire cinema team to deliver superior service to customers
	Develop and nurture internal and external relationships to help achieve business objectives
	Develop initiatives and search for viable opportunities for business and EBITDA growth
	Participate in the development and implement the budget for assigned site
	Maintain extensive knowledge of current market conditions, market share and trends and prepare periodic reports on achievements.


Operations:


	To implement, maintain and ensure adherence to Operations Standards, Company Policies and Procedures and initiatives.
	To deliver excellent Guest service through well trained and highly motivated teams. Ensure that Guest concerns are responded to appropriately and in a timely manner. Ensure all Guest feedback is reviewed and analysed, action plans created where necessary
	To propose and prepare with the filmbooking department, a performance schedule plan to meet business plan objectives.
	To drive daily retail profitability by maximising RPH & minimising shrinkage and wastage. Ensure relevant checks and balances are in place to ensure delivery of KPI's
	To pro-actively manage operational costs on a day-to-day basis through effective management of controllable costs, third party contractors and team members.
	Accountability for the whole employee life cycle of all employees. This includes managing the performance of all employees by cultivating a team spirit, effective coaching and performance management.
	Manage in-cinema's marketing; maximising audiences for films and other events therefore, increasing admission numbers to increase market share of Filmhouse cinemas and achieve financial growth
	Overall responsibility over the safety of employees and guests by complying with company standards, Health and Safety and Environment legislation and meeting all other statutory requirements.
	To manage and operate security systems and processes to prevent loss and to ensure the security of cash, assets, Guests, and employees at all times


Revenue Assurance:


	Responsible for all cash and cash assets at assigned location
	 Responsible for the disbursement and management of petty cash at the site.
	Responsible for cash evacuation/banking as directed. This includes cash preparation for bank pick-up / drop-off and to send report to site accountant for compilation


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in relevant field from an accredited university
	Minimum of 5 years in operations management at a supervisory level (retail experience preferred)

  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Experienced Hospital Manager Admin",
  "description": "Christian Miracle Hospital is an NHIS-accredited fibroid, ovarian cyst, appendicitis, and maternity hospital in Nigeria, established in 1992. We are located at No. 3 Akutu Crescent office, Pascan Jake bus stop opposite Pine Crest School, Enugu, Independence Layout, Enugu state. We offer the best gynaecological, fertility, and maternity services to our clients and patients. Our in-house laboratory is quality assured, and our dispensary stocks quality drugs and other medical consumables. We run antenatal clinics and fertility clinics every Tuesday.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Experienced Hospital Manager Admin

Job Location: Enugu

Mission Statement


	To lead Christian Miracle Hospital in delivering exceptional Christ-centered healthcare while driving business growth, operational excellence, and spiritual transformation of our community through compassionate service and innovative healthcare solutions.


Job Summary


	To oversee the daily operations, financial health, and strategic growth of the hospital.
	You are the &quot;Chief Revenue Officer,&quot; responsible for transforming the hospital into a profitable brand while maintaining high spiritual and clinical standards.


Core Responsibilities
Medical/Operational Leadership:


	Oversee daily hospital operations to ensure smooth workflow and efficient patient care across all departments.
	Ensure compliance with Nigerian healthcare regulations, Enugu state laws and regulations, NHIS accreditation standards, and industry best practices.
	Develop and implement strategic plans for service expansion, quality improvement, and resource optimization.
	Manage financial records, budget preparation, cost control, and revenue generation initiatives.
	Coordinate patient flow management to minimize wait times and maximize patient satisfaction.
	Cost Control: Audit inventory weekly to prevent pilferage of drugs and consumables.
	Staff Performance: Conduct monthly appraisals based on financial targets (KPIs) and patient satisfaction scores.
	Service Excellence: Ensure waiting times are under 20 minutes and the facility is spotless


Business Development &amp; Marketing:


	Develop and execute comprehensive marketing strategies to increase patient acquisition and market share in Enugu and surrounding areas.
	Revenue Generation: Develop and execute a strategic marketing plan to increase monthly patient registration by 20% and surgery bookings by 15%.
	Partnership Management: Secure retainerships with at least 3 new corporate organizations (banks, schools, NGOs) per quarter.
	Lead Conversion: Manage the &quot;Patient Pipeline.&quot; Ensure every enquiry (phone/walk-in) is followed up until they register or book a procedure.


Digital Marketing:


	Oversee the hospital&rsquo;s social media presence, ensuring daily posts of &quot;Testimonies&quot; and health tips to drive engagement.
	Maintain and nurture relationships with referring physicians, corporate clients, community leaders, and religious organizations to generate consistent patient referrals.
	Implement digital marketing initiatives, including social media presence, website optimization, and online patient engagement platforms.
	Organize community health outreach programs, seminars, and health education sessions to build brand awareness and trust.
	Set monthly patient acquisition targets for all departments and monitor performance against KPIs.


Staff Leadership &amp; Christian Values:


	Recruit, train, and mentor a team of born-again Christian staff who align with the hospital&#39;s mission and values.
	Foster a culture of excellence, compassion, and Christ-like service among all hospital personnel.
	Conduct monthly performance evaluations with clear KPIs and provide coaching for underperforming staff.
	Ensure all staff maintain impeccable grooming, professional deportment, and courteous communication with patients and visitors.


Other Responsibilities:


	Identify partnership opportunities
	Develop new relationships in an effort to grow the business and help company&#39;s expansion
	Maintain existing business
	Think critically when planning to assure project success


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs - Monthly)


	Patient acquisition growth rate (minimum 15% month-over-month)
	Revenue generation against budget targets (minimum 95% achievement)
	Patient satisfaction score (minimum 4.5/5.0)
	Staff retention rate (minimum 90%)
	Operational cost control (variance within 5% of budget)
	Number of new corporate partnerships secured (minimum 2 per month)
	Community outreach program participation (minimum 4 events per month)


Person Specifications/Requirements
Essential Qualifications:


	Bachelor&#39;s degree in any field
	Minimum 2 years of experience in management, business development, sales/marketing, and/or healthcare leadership role
	Proven track record in healthcare business development and revenue growth
	Strong understanding of Nigerian healthcare regulations and NHIS operations


Essential Skills &amp; Attributes:


	Born-again Christian with strong moral character and alignment with Christian values
	Exceptional leadership, team management, and conflict resolution skills
	Advanced business development, marketing, and sales abilities
	Proficient in financial management, budgeting, and revenue cycle optimization
	Excellent communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills
	Computer literacy in hospital management systems, MS Office Suite, and digital marketing tools
	Strategic thinking with the ability to convert vision into actionable plans
	Resilient under pressure with strong problem-solving capabilities


Preferred Experience:


	Experience in private hospital management or HMO in Nigeria
	Background in obstetrics/gynaecology or specialized surgical services
	Track record of successful community engagement and stakeholder relationship management
	The ideal candidate will lead initiatives to generate and engage with business partners to build new business for the company. This candidate will be focused and have strong communication skills.
	They should be able to think critically when making plans and have a demonstrated ability to execute a particular strategy.


Qualifications:


	Bachelor&#39;s degree or equivalent experience
	3 Prior industry-related business development experience
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills
	Proven knowledge and execution of successful development strategies
	Focused and goal-oriented
	Please note that due to the high volume of applications we receive, only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV and application letter, clearly stating that they have read and accept the job description, roles, and responsibilities; and why they believe their character, work experience(s), and skills qualify them for this position to: cmhospitalenugunigeria@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

	Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience

	Focused and goal-oriented
	Please note that due to the high volume of applications we receive, only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

title: Job Openings at Sujimoto Group
content:
(adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    

Sujimoto Group is Nigeria's foremost luxury real estate and hospitality brand, widely regarded as the Rolls Royce of luxury real estate in Africa. Founded by Dr. Sijibomi Ogundele, Sujimoto has delivered some of Nigeria's most iconic developments — including LeonardoBySujimoto, LucreziaBySujimoto, GiulianoBySujimoto, and the S-Hotel — across Ikoyi, Banana Island, and Abuja. The Group's hospitality arm, Sujimoto Residences, operates premium shortlet apartments that are consistently fully booked, alongside a restaurant and lounge, all built on the same philosophy that defines every Sujimoto product: we don't do things 10% better — we do things 10 times better. We are now expanding our hospitality team and looking for driven, high-standard professionals who are ready to build something exceptional with us.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) General Manager

Job Location: Ikoyi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Department: Group Operations
Reports to: Group Managing Director

Role Overview


	The General Manager, Operations will serve as the operational backbone of the Sujimoto group. This is a critical leadership appointment for a seasoned executive who brings cross-industry expertise, sharp commercial instincts, and the ability to simultaneously manage complex, multi-subsidiary operations. The GM will be the GMD's principal executor of strategic priorities, translating vision into disciplined operational outcomes across every vertical under the Sujimoto umbrella.
	This role demands a leader who thrives in dynamic, fast-scaling environments, someone who can impose structure without stifling agility, command the respect of subsidiary heads, and make high-stakes decisions with confidence.


Key Responsibilities
Strategic Operations Leadership:


	Serve as the primary operational lead across all Sujimoto subsidiaries, ensuring each business unit operates in alignment with group-wide strategic objectives.
	Develop and implement a unified operational framework that standardises processes, elevates service delivery, and drives efficiency across the conglomerate.
	Partner with the CEO to translate long-term business strategy into actionable operational roadmaps with measurable milestones.
	Conduct regular performance reviews of each subsidiary, identifying gaps, bottlenecks, and opportunities for growth or restructuring.


Multi-Subsidiary Management:


	Provide hands-on leadership and directional oversight to the General Managers and heads of departments within each subsidiary.
	Coordinate cross-subsidiary initiatives including shared services, resource allocation, joint procurement, and group-wide process improvement projects.
	Facilitate seamless communication and collaboration across business units to eliminate silos and align subsidiary goals with holding group priorities.


Financial Oversight & Performance Management:


	Oversee the operational budgets of all subsidiaries, ensuring fiscal discipline, cost optimisation, and return on investment.
	Work with the CFO and subsidiary finance teams to develop and monitor KPIs, operational scorecards, and financial performance dashboards.
	Lead periodic operational audits and enforce accountability at all levels of subsidiary management.


People & Culture:


	Champion a performance-driven, people-first culture across all subsidiaries, in partnership with HR.
	Identify and develop high-potential talent within the group, building a strong internal pipeline for succession.
	Mediate escalated organisational issues and provide decisive leadership during periods of change or uncertainty.


Risk, Compliance & Governance:


	Ensure all subsidiaries comply with applicable regulatory, legal, and corporate governance standards.
	Identify operational risks across the group and develop robust mitigation frameworks.
	Represent the holding group in high-level stakeholder engagements, partnerships, and regulatory interactions where required.


Qualifications and Experience


	Bachelor's Degree in Business Administration, Engineering, Management, or a related field.
	Minimum of 7 years of progressive management experience, with at least 3years in a senior operations role spanning multiple business units or industries.
	Demonstrated track record of leading and scaling operations in complex, multi-subsidiary or conglomerate environments.
	Cross-industry experience is strongly preferred — candidates with exposure to real estate, construction, hospitality, FMCG, financial services, or professional services are particularly well-suited.
	A postgraduate degree or certification isan advantage.
	Strong financial acumen, with experience managing large operational budgets and driving cost efficiency.
	Proven ability to build, manage, and inspire high-performing leadership teams.
	Familiarity with Nigerian business environments, regulatory frameworks, and stakeholder ecosystems is essential.


Core Competencies:


	Strategic Thinking & Execution
	Cross-Functional Leadership
	Operational Excellence
	Financial Acumen
	Stakeholder Management
	Change Management
	Conflict Resolution
	Data-Driven Decision Making
	Executive Presence
	Resilience & Adaptability.


What we Offer


	A competitive executive compensation package benchmarked against industry peers.
	The opportunity to operate at the epicentre of one of Lagos' most dynamic and ambitious conglomerates.
	A direct seat at the executive table, with meaningful influence over the direction and growth of a premium business group.
	Exposure to high-value projects and a network of exceptional professionals across multiple industries.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their updated CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

 

 

2.) Executive Assistant to the GMD 

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Department: Office of the GMD
Reports to: Group Managing Director

Role Overview


	We are seeking an extraordinarily capable Executive Assistant who will serve not only as the GMD's organisational anchor, but as a strategic partner — someone who functions, in practice, as a Chief of Staff. This is not a conventional EA role. The right candidate will be someone who has operated at the highest levels of executive support and understands that true effectiveness in this position requires commercial acumen, discretion, strategic thinking, and the confidence to act decisively in the GMD's stead when required.
	You will manage the full scope of the GMD's professional world — from orchestrating a complex schedule and driving cross-functional follow-through, to representing the GMD in internal and external engagements and ensuring that no strategic priority falls through the cracks.
	You will be the GMD's eyes and ears across the organisation, and the invisible engine that keeps the executive office running with precision.


Key Responsibilities
Executive Office Management:


	Manage the GMD's calendar with a high degree of intelligence — not just scheduling meetings, but making informed decisions about what warrants the GMD's time and attention.
	Oversee all executive correspondence, ensuring timely, professional, and appropriately prioritised responses on behalf of the GMD.
	Coordinate complex domestic and international travel logistics including itineraries, visas, accommodation, ground transport, and meeting briefings.
	Prepare the GMD for meetings, engagements, and presentations by compiling comprehensive briefs, background notes, and talking points.


Chief of Staff Functions:


	Track and follow up on all strategic action items, commitments, and deliverables emanating from executive meetings, ensuring accountability across the leadership team.
	Act as a trusted liaison between the GMD and internal stakeholders — subsidiary heads, department leads, board members, and external partners.
	Attend leadership meetings, take high-quality minutes, and ensure the accurate translation of decisions into trackable actions.
	Manage the GMD's office budget, discretionary expenses, and vendor relationships related to the executive office.
	Proactively identify gaps in the GMD's agenda and flag risks or issues before they escalate, offering well-considered solutions.


Strategic & Project Support:


	Support the GMD in the preparation of board presentations, investor decks, strategic reports, and executive communications.
	Conduct research, competitive intelligence, and data synthesis on business, market, and industry topics as required by the GMD.
	Manage and drive special projects assigned by the GMD, coordinating cross-functional teams and reporting on progress.
	Maintain a running awareness of group-wide strategic priorities and flag key milestones, risks, and dependencies to the GMD in a timely manner.


Stakeholder & Relationship Management:


	Serve as the first point of contact for high-value stakeholders, investors, and VIP guests engaging with the GMD's office.
	Build and maintain strong professional relationships with key internal and external contacts, representing the GMD's office with discretion and authority.
	Manage confidential information with absolute integrity and exercise sound judgement on all matters of sensitivity.


Qualifications and Experience


	A Bachelor's degree is required; a postgraduate qualification in Business Administration, Management, or a related field is a significant advantage.
	Minimum of 7 years of experience in executive support, with at least 3 years providing C-suite-level EA or Chief of Staff support in a structured organisation.
	Experience in a fast-paced, high-growth, or conglomerate environment is strongly preferred.
	Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, you will write and speak on behalf of the GMD, and the quality of your communication must reflect that.
	Demonstrated ability to manage complex, competing priorities under pressure without compromising on quality or professionalism.
	High proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook) and project/task management tools.
	Impeccable personal presentation and professional conduct befitting a premium executive environment.
	A track record of building strong, trusted relationships with senior executives and external stakeholders.


Personal Attributes:


	Exceptional Discretion & Confidentiality
	Proactive Problem Solving
	High Emotional Intelligence
	Meticulous Attention to Detail
	Outstanding Communication Skills
	Commercial & Strategic Awareness
	Grace Under Pressure
	Initiative & Self-Direction
	Impeccable Professionalism
	Loyalty & Dependability.


What we Offer


	An unparalleled seat at the centre of a premium, fast-growing conglomerate.
	Exposure to strategic decision-making at the highest level of an ambitious Nigerian enterprise.
	A competitive compensation package that reflects the significance and demands of this role.
	A structured, high-performance environment where excellence is both expected and rewarded.
	The opportunity to grow into a substantive leadership or operational role as the organisation expands.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their updated CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: This role requires someone who does not need to be told what to do. If you are the kind of professional who anticipates, prepares, and executes with quiet excellence, we want to hear from you.

 

 

3.)  Freelance Graphics Designer

Job Location: Lagos 
Employment Type: Contract

Description


	We are building a pool of reliable freelance graphic designers to support our in-house media and marketing team on an ongoing, per-project basis.


???????????????? ????????????'???????? ???????? ???????????????????????????? ????????


	Social media creatives (Instagram, LinkedIn, Twitter)
	Property flyers, brochures & marketing collateral
	Event graphics & promotional materials
	Brand-consistent design assets across multiple formats


???????????????? ????????'???????? ???????????????????????????? ????????????


	Strong portfolio — show us your best 5–10 pieces
	Proficiency in Canva, Adobe Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign), or Figma
	Speed — most briefs will require delivery within 24–48 hours
	Ability to work with brand guidelines and maintain visual consistency
	Responsive communication (WhatsApp availability is a must)


????????????????????????????


	N5,000 – N20,000 per design, depending on complexity.
	Payment is per approved deliverable — not hourly.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their name and location, Portfolio link or 5–10 samples of your work (PDF or link), Tools you use, typical turnaround time for a single social media graphic and rate range within N5,000 – N20,000  to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted designers will be

	Build and maintain strong professional relationships with key internal and external contacts, representing the GMD's office with discretion and authority.
	Manage confidential information with absolute integrity and exercise sound judgement on all matters of sensitivity.


Qualifications and Experience


	A Bachelor's degree is required; a postgraduate qualification in Business Administration, Management, or a related field is a significant advantage.
	Minimum of 7 years of experience in executive support, with at least 3 years providing C-suite-level EA or Chief of Staff support in a structured organisation.
	Experience in a fast-paced, high-growth, or conglomerate environment is strongly preferred.
	Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, you will write and speak on behalf of the GMD, and the quality of your communication must reflect that.
	Demonstrated ability to manage complex, competing priorities under pressure without compromising on quality or professionalism.
	High proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook) and project/task management tools.
	Impeccable personal presentation and professional conduct befitting a premium executive environment.
	A track record of building strong, trusted relationships with senior executives and external stakeholders.


Personal Attributes:


	Exceptional Discretion & Confidentiality
	Proactive Problem Solving
	High Emotional Intelligence
	Meticulous Attention to Detail
	Outstanding Communication Skills
	Commercial & Strategic Awareness
	Grace Under Pressure
	Initiative & Self-Direction
	Impeccable Professionalism
	Loyalty & Dependability.


What we Offer


	An unparalleled seat at the centre of a premium, fast-growing conglomerate.
	Exposure to strategic decision-making at the highest level of an ambitious Nigerian enterprise.
	A competitive compensation package that reflects the significance and demands of this role.
	A structured, high-performance environment where excellence is both expected and rewarded.
	The opportunity to grow into a substantive leadership or operational role as the organisation expands.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their updated CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: This role requires someone who does not need to be told what to do. If you are the kind of professional who anticipates, prepares, and executes with quiet excellence, we want to hear from

HRIS & People Analyst Job at PZ Cussons




        
    

        
        PZ Cussons is a dynamic consumer products group and innovator of some of the world's best known and loved brands. Calling on over 130 years of expertise, we operate internationally in carefully selected developed and emerging markets that present the greatest strategic potential for future growth. Our world-class supply chain and distribution networks enable us to meet our global consumer needs and deliver quality brands that add value and enhance everyday lives.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HRIS & People Analyst

Job ID: JR002871
Job Location: Ilupeju, Lagos

Responsibilities
Headcount Reporting & Analytics:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Generate and submit monthly headcount reports by the 8th of every month.
	Develop analytics dashboards with insights on employee trends, turnover, and workforce metrics.
	Share insights with management to support data-driven decision-making.


 Audit:


	Conduct periodic pre-audit checks to ensure accurate documentation and readiness for internal and external audits.
	Maintain compliance with organizational policies, statutory regulations, and audit requirements.
	Track, log, and resolve all audit issues within one week of notice.
	Maintain an audit tracker with action plans and resolution timelines.


 Payroll Support:


	Compile and deliver monthly payroll input to the Payroll Team by the 10th of each month.
	Prepare payroll inputs by compiling overtime data for across SBUs.
	Process and include sales bonuses and productivity bonuses from SBUs
	Ensure accuracy of all payroll-related data before submission.
	Provide clarification to employees on payroll-related issues when required.


 Employee Records & Filing:


	Supervise completion of new employee filing by the 15th of the new month.
	Maintain accurate and up-to-date employee digital and physical files.
	Ensure all manager files across are completed (both physical and digital) within set timelines.
	Regularly audit employee files for accuracy and completeness.


 Policy Development, Review & Documentation:


	Review, develop, and update HR policies to reflect evolving business needs and compliance requirements as needed.
	Maintain a central repository for all HR policies and ensure easy access.


 Business Support (SBU Support & Projects):


	Support business projects as required by Head of Reward and shared services.
	Provide HR support on cross-functional projects as required.
